channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden
item.description: OPM’s Dan Jacobs and stackArmor’s Gaurav “GP” Pal led an ACT-IAC team to develop a maturity model to help implement authority to operate as code. The post How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Akka distributed computing platform adds Java SDK
item.description: Akka, formerly known as Lightbend, has released Akka 3, an update to the JVM-based distributed computing platform that adds a Java SDK as well as serverless and “bring your own cloud” deployment options. Akka 3 and the company’s name change were announced November 15.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data-driven startup Aggie uses AI to manage small business social media
item.description: A new, AI-powered platform called Aggie wants to manage your brand’s social media. Aggie is a product of Audience Genomics, which has since 2018 offered audience data analytics to big brands like Universal Studios, AMC Television, and Fenty Beauty.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Trump’s pick for FCC chairman is Brendan Carr
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Sunday named Brendan Carr, the senior Republican on the Federal Communications Commission, as the new chairman of the agency tasked with regulating broadcasting, telecommunications, and broadband.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Energy Department's ETAC Cyber Threat Center Goes Operational
item.description: The Energy Department's Energy Threat Analysis Center (ETAC) has transitioned to steady-state operations, marking a significant step in its efforts to combat cyber threats to the energy sector.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Beyond the checkbox: Building confidence in your DR strategy
item.description: Disaster recovery (DR) is often no more than a checkbox item for IT, because the team spends so much time fighting fires and working to advance new, mission-critical implementations. To that point, DR didn’t make CIOs’ top five priorities in the Foundry State of the CIO 2024 survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Vaio SX14-R: A Lightweight Business Laptop with Advanced Connectivity Options
item.description: Vaio's new SX14-R laptop offers a powerful Intel Core Ultra H-series processor, advanced connectivity options, and sleek color choices.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How Trump’s appointment of Brendan Carr as FCC chairman will affect enterprises
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has announced Brendan Carr as the next head of the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC), the organization responsible for setting the regulations governing telecoms, broadband, satellite communications and broadcasting in the US.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: El Capitan bumps Frontier to claim world’s fastest supercomputer title
item.description: The new El Capitan system at the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California made its debut at No. 1 on the latest TOP500 list of the world’s most powerful supercomputers, released today. The Frontier system at Oak Ridge National Laboratory in Tennessee moved down to the No.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: En el camino hacia la eficiencia digital los bancos apuestan por la IA
item.description: Ante la reducción de las partidas presupuestarias y el aumento de las expectativas de los clientes, los bancos recurren cada vez más a la inteligencia artificial (IA), según un estudio reciente de la consultora Publicis Sapiens.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: T-Mobile hacked by Chinese cyber espionage in major attack on US telecoms
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about T-Mobile being hacked in broad cyberattack on global phone and internet companies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IBM announces 50-fold quantum speed improvement
item.description: IBM launched its most advanced quantum computer yet last week at its inaugural quantum developer conference. It features nearly twice the gates of last year’s quantum utility demonstration – and a 50-fold speed increase.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Prosegur se apoya en la IA para optimizar y agilizar los procesos de selección
item.description: Desde su constitución en el año 1976, la compañía de seguridad Prosegur ha basado su crecimiento y evolución en una estrategia sustentada en tres pilares fundamentales: la innovación, su equipo humano y una orientación al cliente.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The dirty little secret of open source contributions
item.description: “Nobody cares if you contribute.” That’s what a Postgres friend said to me during lunch at KubeCon when I suggested that hiring Postgres contributors could be a selling point for customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The White House Finalizes $6.6 Billion Grant Agreement with TSMC for Arizona Facilities
item.description: The White House announces a finalized agreement with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) to invest billions in facilities in Arizona under the Chips and Science Act.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: AI is dumber than you think
item.description: OpenAI recently introduced SimpleQA, a new benchmark for evaluating the factual accuracy of large language models (LLMs) that underpin generative AI (genAI).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: First combined AI-RAN network from Nvidia and SoftBank supports inferencing, claims return of $5 for every $1 invested
item.description: AI inferencing requires an incredible amount of network traffic, and telcos must have the ability to keep up with that extreme demand. However, it’s estimated that traditional networks only use one-third of their capacity due to over-provisioning for peak loads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Polymarket at center of political storm after reported FBI raid
item.description: Prediction betting market Polymarket and its founder and CEO Shayne Coplan have found themselves at the center of a debate about political betting after the FBI reportedly raided Coplan’s home on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Solidigm unveils 122TB data center SSD for AI workloads
item.description: Solidigm, a provider of NAND flash solid-state drives, and memory giant Micron have each introduced very high-capacity SSD drives meant for enterprise data centers, particularly in AI use cases. Traditional hard disk drives have maintained their viability because of capacity advantages over SSDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cloud creates network blind spots, complicates problem resolution
item.description: As network environments become more complex, network teams struggle to gain visibility across internet and cloud components, citing blind spots and a lack of skilled workers as contributing factors to slow problem resolution, according to a Broadcom report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: All agencies to give grant funding opportunities a facelift
item.description: A year since it launched, the Council of Federal Financial Assistance already is improving the transparency, accountability and results of grant funding. The post All agencies to give grant funding opportunities a facelift first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
